evals.prompt: Build me a Web Clipper template for Substack articles. I keep clips in Clippings/Substack, and I always tag things with #newsletter.
evals.expected_output: A structurally valid Web Clipper template JSON targeting Substack article URLs, with no unresolved REPLACE_ME tokens, author/published extracted via schema or meta before falling back to selectors, the newsletter tag present, and path set to Clippings/Substack.
assertions: The output is a single JSON file
JSON parses without error and properties is an array, not an object
No REPLACE_ME token remains anywhere in the output
triggers matches substack.com article URLs and excludes the homepage/feed/settings pages
A tag or property containing 'newsletter' is present
author and published are extracted via schema or meta variables, not a raw CSS selector, unless the template notes schema/meta were unavailable

evals.prompt: hey can you make a clipper template for youtube videos, i want the transcript in the note not just a link
evals.expected_output: A template targeting YouTube video URLs whose noteContentFormat includes transcript content, not just {{content}} or a bare link.
assertions: No REPLACE_ME token remains anywhere in the output
triggers matches YouTube watch-page URLs and excludes the YouTube homepage/search/channel-list pages
noteContentFormat includes transcript-related content, not just a bare {{content}} or link

evals.prompt: Give me a template for some random blog at obscurewriter.example — it's not a platform you'd know, just look at the page and figure it out.
evals.expected_output: A template for an unlisted platform, produced by inspecting the live page's schema/meta/selectors rather than guessing, with every extraction field resolved to a real field and no REPLACE_ME left over — or an explicit statement that the page couldn't be inspected and manual selector input is needed.
assertions: JSON parses without error, or the skill explicitly asks for selector/page details instead of guessing
No REPLACE_ME token remains anywhere in the delivered output
The skill's response references inspecting the actual page (schema, meta, or selector) rather than inventing values with no basis
